name: Yucatán (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Yucatán centred.
history: Yucatán has no nationally recognised official state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state. (A separate historical flag of the short-lived Republic of Yucatán of the 1840s exists but is not a current state flag.)

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.
The arms reference the Maya heritage of the peninsula, the henequen agriculture that long dominated the state's economy, the deer of the Yucatecan landscape, and the colonial foundation of Mérida.
